prompt: Generate a photorealistic aerial cinematic image, minimum 1024x1024 resolution, saved as image_5_latest.jpg: A dense Amazon rainforest shot from high above, showing the distinctive FISHBONE pattern of illegal logging — a central road cutting deep into the forest with smaller roads branching off like fish bones, each branch leaving behind a corridor of cleared, burned brown earth surrounded by rich green canopy. The contrast between the living green and the dead brown fishbone scar should feel visceral and surgical — like a wound being methodically cut into living tissue. Golden hour light, smoke rising from the freshest cuts, photorealistic, 8K quality, no text, no labels, no people, no charts.
dataset_name: Global Forest Watch - Primary Forest Loss
insight: Amazon deforestation follows a systematic fishbone pattern — roads carve methodically into living forest, each branch leaving behind skeletal corridors of burned earth that spread like a parasitic infection through ancient canopy.
